Title: A large-scale gene regulatory network for rice endosperm starch biosynthesis and its application in genetic improvement of rice quality

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of rice eating and cooking quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced OsGBSSI expression/protein lowers amylose content and improves eating and cooking quality; severe reduction also affects grain appearance depending on regulator/background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; immunoblot; regulatory-network mapping; CRISPR knockout of upstream TFs; metabolite/quality profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for amylose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in developing endosperm; assayed at 10 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA enhances upstream OsABI5/OsERF44-mediated activation indirectly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong downregulation in osabi5/oserf44 is associated with increased chalkiness, lower grain thickness, and reduced grain weight; moderate downregulation via selected TF knockout can improve ECQ with limited penalties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGBSSI is the key amylose synthesis enzyme gene targeted by 39 TFs in the constructed endosperm starch regulatory network. 2. OsERF44 directly activates its promoter, while OsABI5 activates OsERF44 in an ABA-responsive pathway. 3. Knockout of several OsGBSSI-targeting TFs moderately reduces OsGBSSI expression and amylose content, improving rice eating and cooking quality.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning OsGBSSI transcription via editing upstream TFs offers a practical route to create soft rice with improved eating quality, especially in Wx^b backgrounds, while avoiding complete waxy phenotypes.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major starch component affecting grain physicochemical quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression in oserf44 and osabi5 contributes to lower starch accumulation and altered grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; mutant phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced expression is associated with starch deficiency, increased chalkiness, and lower grain weight in upstream regulator mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSSI is one of five SSEGs directly bound and activated by OsERF44. 2. Its transactivation is enhanced by OsABI5 and ABA through the OsABI5-OsERF44 pathway. 3. Expression and protein abundance are reduced in oserf44 and osabi5 mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily a mechanistic node in the ABA-OsABI5-OsERF44 starch regulatory module; direct suppression may impair grain filling, so upstream fine-tuning is preferable.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major starch component affecting grain quality and starch granule architecture
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for proper assembly of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression in oserf44 and osabi5 is associated with defective starch granule packing and reduced starch accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; mutant phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Downregulation accompanies increased chalkiness and lower grain weight in upstream regulator mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsISA2 is directly activated by OsERF44. 2. Its transcript and protein levels decline in oserf44 and osabi5 mutants. 3. It is part of the ABA-OsABI5-OsERF44-SSEGs module controlling endosperm starch biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a network readout gene; direct perturbation is likely detrimental to starch structure and grain quality.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary carbon reserve determining grain filling and quality
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for proper assembly/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression/protein in oserf44 and osabi5 accompanies decreased starch accumulation and poorer grain appearance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; mutant phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced activity in the upstream regulator mutants is associated with irregular, loosely packed starch granules and yield penalty
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsISA3 is one of five SSEGs directly bound and activated by OsERF44. 2. Its abundance is reduced in both oserf44 and osabi5. 3. It contributes to the starch-deficient phenotype of the mutants as part of the downstream module.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a primary breeding target here; best considered as a downstream indicator of regulatory perturbation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major starch component influencing gelatinization and grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression/protein in oserf44 and osabi5 contributes to impaired starch biosynthesis and poor grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; mutant phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower expression is associated with irregular starch granules, higher chalkiness, and reduced grain weight in regulator mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsBEIIb is directly targeted and activated by OsERF44. 2. Its expression and protein abundance are reduced in oserf44 and osabi5. 3. It functions within the downstream SSEG module of the ABA-OsABI5-OsERF44 pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important network output gene, but direct disruption may compromise starch structure and grain quality.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon reserve determining grain filling, rice appearance, and eating/cooking quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsGBSSI, OsISA3, OsISA2, OsBEIIb, and OsSSI by binding their promoters via a dehydrin cis-motif
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Node regulator directly connecting ABA signaling to five starch synthesis enzyme genes; important positive controller of endosperm starch biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward input from ABA/OsABI5 to downstream starch enzyme genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsERF44 decreases total starch, amylose, and amylopectin, increases chalkiness, and reduces grain thickness and grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA pull-down/LC-MS; Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; CRISPR knockout mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; grain quality phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsABI5 (genetic upstream regulator, not protein-protein interaction demonstrated)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function analyzed in developing endosperm; downstream target genes measured in 10 DAF seeds
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA enhances OsABI5-mediated activation and boosts OsERF44-dependent transactivation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes severe starch deficiency, poor appearance quality, and lower grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsERF44 is a newly identified AP2/ERF node TF in the rice endosperm starch regulatory network. 2. It directly binds dehydrin cis-motifs in promoters of five SSEGs and activates their expression. 3. Loss of OsERF44 causes lower starch accumulation, increased chalkiness, and reduced grain weight.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A valuable positive regulator for improving starch accumulation, but knockout is deleterious; careful promoter engineering or allele tuning rather than loss-of-function would be needed.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsERF44 acts downstream of OsABI5, but OsABI5 can still activate OsGBSSI in the absence of OsERF44, indicating parallel ABA-responsive branches.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor; ABA signaling component
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon reserve affecting grain filling and rice quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsERF44 by binding ABRE-box motifs in its promoter; also can activate OsbZIP58 according to supplementary evidence
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ABA signaling TF linking ABA input to starch synthesis regulatory cascades in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: ABA-induced feedforward activation of downstream starch regulators
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsABI5 reduces expression of OsERF44 and five downstream SSEGs, lowering total starch, amylose, and amylopectin and worsening chalkiness and grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC; CRISPR knockout mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; grain quality phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assessed in developing endosperm; 10 DAF seeds analyzed
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout impairs starch accumulation and grain quality/yield traits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsABI5 binds ABRE motifs in the OsERF44 promoter and activates its expression. 2. ABA enhances OsABI5-mediated activation and the downstream activation of SSEG promoters. 3. osabi5 mutants show reduced starch accumulation and poorer grain appearance and weight.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for enhancing grain filling through positive tuning of ABA-responsive starch regulation; knockout is unsuitable because it penalizes yield and quality.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsABI5 does not directly bind the OsGBSSI promoter, indicating indirect regulation through OsERF44 and other TFs such as OsbZIP58.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key determinant of rice eating and cooking quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional regulation of OsGBSSI inferred from knockout phenotype and promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Knockout reduces OsGBSSI transcript and protein abundance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Practical tunable regulator of amylose level with favorable grain appearance outcome, especially in Wx^b background
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout moderately lowers amylose content and improves eating/cooking quality; in Nipponbare it does so without significantly worsening chalkiness; in YK17 it partially improves ECQ and reduces chalkiness
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA pull-down/LC-MS; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in Nipponbare and Zhongjiazao17; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; grain quality phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSPL7 is one of the OsGBSSI-binding TFs identified in the regulatory network. 2. CRISPR knockout lowers OsGBSSI expression/protein and reduces amylose content. 3. In Nipponbare and YK17 backgrounds, knockout improves ECQ, with especially favorable appearance in Nipponbare and reduced chalkiness in YK17.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding soft rice by genome editing, especially in medium-amylose Wx^b backgrounds where ECQ can be improved without major appearance penalties.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The article notes no previous report that OsSPL7 directly binds the OsGBSSI promoter before this study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key determinant of rice eating and cooking quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional regulation of OsGBSSI inferred from promoter binding and knockout phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Knockout reduces OsGBSSI transcript and protein abundance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Practical regulator enabling moderate amylose reduction with improved ECQ and acceptable appearance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout moderately lowers amylose content and improves eating/cooking quality; in Nipponbare it does not significantly increase chalkiness; in YK17 it lowers amylose and chalkiness with partial ECQ improvement
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA pull-down/LC-MS; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in Nipponbare and Zhongjiazao17; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; grain quality phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slight decrease in 1000-grain weight; slight increase in endosperm protein content and chewiness in YK17 osb3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsB3 is an OsGBSSI-targeting TF identified from the starch regulatory network. 2. Knockout reduces OsGBSSI expression/protein and lowers amylose content. 3. It is a promising editing target for improving ECQ while maintaining good appearance in japonica and partially improving quality in indica.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent candidate for rapid genome editing to create soft rice germplasm with favorable ECQ and appearance, particularly under medium-amylose Wx^b backgrounds.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No previous report had shown direct binding of OsB3 to OsGBSSI before this study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key determinant of rice eating and cooking quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional regulation of OsGBSSI inferred from knockout phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Knockout reduces OsGBSSI transcript and protein abundance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to practical tuning of amylose content but with appearance-quality cost
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout lowers amylose content and improves ECQ, but increases chalky grain rate/chalkiness in Nipponbare
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA pull-down/LC-MS; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; grain quality phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher chalkiness and slight reduction in 1000-grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMADS91 targets OsGBSSI in the network. 2. Knockout lowers OsGBSSI expression and amylose content. 3. Although ECQ improves, appearance quality worsens, limiting breeding utility.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible editing target for reducing amylose, but less favorable than OsSPL7 or OsB3 because of chalkiness penalty.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key determinant of rice eating and cooking quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional regulation of OsGBSSI inferred from knockout phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Knockout reduces OsGBSSI transcript and protein abundance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to amylose tuning but with unfavorable appearance impact
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout lowers amylose content and improves ECQ, but significantly increases chalkiness in Nipponbare
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA pull-down/LC-MS; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; grain quality phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher chalkiness and slight reduction in 1000-grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsC2H2 is an OsGBSSI-targeting TF identified in the network. 2. Knockout reduces OsGBSSI expression and amylose content. 3. The resulting appearance penalty makes it a weaker breeding target than OsSPL7 or OsB3.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used to lower amylose, but breeding deployment is limited by increased chalkiness.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key determinant of rice eating and cooking quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional regulation of OsGBSSI inferred from knockout phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Knockout reduces OsGBSSI transcript and protein abundance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to amylose tuning but less favorable due to appearance penalty
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout lowers amylose content and improves ECQ, but significantly increases chalkiness in Nipponbare
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA pull-down/LC-MS; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; grain quality phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not specifically stated as highly expressed in developing endosperm relative to the other four; differential tissue expression reported
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher chalkiness and slight reduction in 1000-grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB1 is an OsGBSSI-targeting TF from the network. 2. Knockout reduces OsGBSSI expression/protein and lowers amylose content. 3. Despite ECQ improvement, appearance quality deteriorates, reducing application value.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tuning target, but inferior to OsSPL7 and OsB3 for practical soft-rice breeding.
